question: Why halogens have maximum negative electron gain enthalpy?
answer: Halogens have seven electrons in their valence shell and require only one electron to attain noble gas configuration. Hence maximum negative electron gain enthalpy.

question: Why fluorine shows anomalous behaviour as compared to other halogens?
answer: (i) Smallest size (ii) Very high electronegativity (iii) Absence of d-orbitals (iv) Low dissociation enthalpy in molecular form.

question: Arrange the hydrogen halides (HF to HI) in the decreasing order of their reducing character.
Why fluorine is a stronger oxidizing agent than chlorine?
answer: Fluorine has greater E° value (2.87V) than chlorine (1.36V).

question: What are the sizes of X and X' in the interhalogen compounds?
answer: Size of X is greater than X'.

question: Name the cell used in hearing aids and watches.
How much charge in terms of Faraday is required to reduce one mol of MnO<sub>4</sub><sup>-</sup> to Mn<sup>2+</sup>?
answer: 5F. MnO<sub>4</sub><sup>-</sup> + 5e<sup>-</sup> -> Mn<sup>2+</sup>. Charge = 5 x 96500 = 4.825 x 10<sup>5</sup> C

options: Both correct, R does not explain A
answer: (d) A is wrong, R is correct.

question: Assertion (A): The C-O-C bond angle in ethers is slightly less than tetrahedral angle. Reason (R): Due to repulsive interaction between two alkyl groups.
options: Both correct, R does not explain A

question: Assertion (A): Low spin tetrahedral complexes are rarely observed. Reason (R): Crystal field splitting energy is less than pairing energy for tetrahedral complexes.
options: Both correct, R does not explain A

question: Assertion (A): Elevation in boiling point is a colligative property. Reason (R): Elevation in boiling point is directly proportional to molality.
options: Both correct, R does not explain A

question: Assertion (A): Oxidation of ketones is easier than aldehydes. Reason (R): C-C bond of ketones is stronger than C-H bond of aldehydes.
options: Both correct, R does not explain A
answer: (d) A is wrong, R is correct.

question: State Raoult's law for a solution containing volatile components. What is the similarity between Raoult's law and Henry's law?
answer: Raoult's law: Partial pressure of volatile component is directly proportional to mole fraction. Similarity: Both relate partial pressure to mole fraction. P<sub>A</sub> = K<sub>x</sub>A (Raoult's) and P<sub>A</sub> = K<sub>H</sub>x<sub>A</sub> (Henry's).

question: Write the role of (a) Dilute NaCN in extraction of Gold. (b) CO in extraction of Iron. OR How is leaching carried out for low grade copper ores? Name the method for refining copper.
answer: (a) NaCN: Leaching agent for gold. (b) CO: Reducing agent in blast furnace. OR Acid leaching with H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>. Refining: Electrolytic refining.

question: Define adsorption with an example. Role in heterogeneous catalysis? OR Define Brownian movement, its cause and role in colloidal stability.
answer: Adsorption: Accumulation at surface. E.g., gas on charcoal. Lowers activation energy. OR Brownian movement: Zigzag motion from unequal bombardment. Prevents settling.

question: (a) IUPAC name and hybridisation of [Fe(CN)<sub>6</sub>]<sup>3-</sup> (Fe=26). (b) Difference between ambidentate and chelating ligand.
answer: (a) Hexacyanoferrate(III), d<sup>2</sup>sp<sup>3</sup>. (b) Ambidentate: bonds through different atoms (NO<sub>2</sub><sup>-</sup>). Chelating: multiple donor groups forming ring (en).

question: How do antiseptics differ from disinfectants? Name a substance which can be used as both.
answer: Antiseptics: living tissues. Disinfectants: inanimate objects. Phenol: 0.2% antiseptic, 1% disinfectant.

question: Identify monomers in polymers: (i) PET (ii) Nylon 6,6
answer: (i) Ethylene glycol + Terephthalic acid. (ii) Hexamethylenediamine + Adipic acid.

question: Draw structures of: (i) H<sub>2</sub>S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>8</sub> (ii) XeF<sub>6</sub>
answer: (i) Two SO<sub>4</sub> tetrahedra with O-O peroxo bond. (ii) Distorted octahedral.

question: A 0.01 m aqueous solution of AlCl<sub>3</sub> freezes at -0.068°C. Calculate percentage of dissociation. [K<sub>f</sub> = 1.86 K kg mol<sup>-1</sup>]
Steady current 2A through ZnSO<sub>4</sub> and CuSO<sub>4</sub> cells in series, 2g Cu deposited. Find time and mass of Zn. [Cu=63.5, Zn=65; 1F=96500]
Differentiate: (i) Amylose vs Amylopectin (ii) Globular vs Fibrous protein (iii) Nucleotide vs Nucleoside
answer: (i) Amylose: straight chain, 1,4-glycosidic. Amylopectin: branched, 1,4 and 1,6. (ii) Globular: coiled, soluble. Fibrous: parallel, insoluble. (iii) Nucleotide: base+sugar+phosphate. Nucleoside: base+sugar.

question: Identify A-F: CH<sub>3</sub>CH(CH<sub>3</sub>)CH<sub>2</sub>Br with alc.KOH, HBr, Na/ether, Mg/ether, H<sub>2</sub>O, NaOEt
answer: A: 2-methylpropene. B: 2-bromo-2-methylpropane. C: 2,2,3,3-tetramethylbutane. D: Grignard reagent. E: 2-methylpropane. F: ethyl isobutyl ether.

question: Products: (i) Propene hydroboration-oxidation (ii) (CH<sub>3</sub>)<sub>3</sub>COH dehydration (iii) Benzyl phenyl ether + HI. OR Convert: (i) Phenol to o-hydroxybenzaldehyde (ii) Methanal to ethanol (iii) Phenol to phenyl ethanoate
answer: (i) Propan-1-ol. (ii) 2-methylpropene. (iii) PhOH + PhCH<sub>2</sub>I. OR (i) Reimer-Tiemann. (ii) CH<sub>3</sub>MgBr + HCHO. (iii) (CH<sub>3</sub>CO)<sub>2</sub>O.

question: Give reasons: (i) Aniline no Friedel-Crafts. (ii) Gabriel's phthalimide not for aromatic amines. (iii) Aliphatic amines stronger bases than NH<sub>3</sub>.
answer: (i) Forms complex with AlCl<sub>3</sub>. (ii) Aryl halides unreactive with potassium phthalimide. (iii) +I effect of alkyl groups.

question: Three differences: lyophobic vs lyophilic sol. OR Define: (i) Protective colloid (ii) Zeta potential (iii) Emulsifying agent
answer: Lyophobic: less stable, irreversible, easily coagulated. Lyophilic: more stable, reversible, resistant to coagulation. OR (i) Lyophilic colloid protecting lyophobic. (ii) Potential between fixed/diffused layers. (iii) Stabilizer for emulsions.

question: (a) Reasons: (i) Transition metals catalytic (ii) Lanthanoid separation difficult (iii) Zn,Cd,Hg soft/low mp. (b) Prepare Na<sub>2</sub>Cr<sub>2</sub>O<sub>7</sub> and K<sub>2</sub>MnO<sub>4</sub>. OR (a) Ti<sup>3+</sup> coloured/Sc<sup>3+</sup> colourless; Cr<sup>2+</sup> reducing agent. (ii) d<sup>4</sup> to stable d<sup>3</sup>. (b) +3 state, strong reducers. (c) 2MnO<sub>4</sub><sup>-</sup> + MnO<sub>2</sub> + 2H<sub>2</sub>O

question: (a) First order 25% in 40 min. Find k and time for 80%. (b) Define order. Pseudo first order condition. (c) Order vs molecularity. OR (a) 50% in 30min at 300K, 10min at 320K. Find E<sub>a</sub>. (b) Effective collision conditions. (c) Same.
answer: (a) k=7.196x10<sup>-3</sup> min<sup>-1</sup>, t=223.7 min. (b) Sum of powers in rate law. One reactant in large excess. (c) Molecularity irrelevant for complex rxns. OR (a) E<sub>a</sub>=43.85 kJ/mol. (b) Sufficient energy, proper orientation.
